steps.title: Recognize the logical gap in “entirely attributable”
steps.content: The student claims the reduction is “entirely” due to lizards. To weaken an exclusive causal claim, you need evidence the same outcome also occurs without that cause.

steps.title: Identify the weakening data
steps.content:
The graph shows the no-lizards enclosure also declined substantially (from ~85 to ~42 by day 30), proving factors other than lizards drive population loss, which contradicts the “entirely attributable to lizards” claim.

  • The starting-count detail is just baseline data.
  • The timing detail is unrelated to the causal claim.
  • The lower final count in the lizard enclosure actually supports the student's conclusion.

The weakening evidence is that the spider population also declined without lizards, so B is correct.
